'''Danny Walker''' (born 29 June 1999) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] and both {{rlnt|England}} and the [[England Knights]] at international level.

He played for the [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]], and has spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[2019 RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Walker was born in [[Warrington]], Cheshire, England.
He was educated at [[Beamont Collegiate Academy]], Warrington.{{cn|date=October 2022}}

==Career==
===Widnes Vikings===
In 2017 he made his [[Super League]] début for the [[Widnes Vikings]] against the [[Leigh Centurions]].{{cn|date=October 2022}}

===Warrington Wolves===
After the conclusion of Season 2018 where the Vikings were relegated, he subsequently signed a three-year deal with his home town club [[Warrington Wolves]] starting from the 2019 season.<ref>{{cite news|title=Danny Walker: Warrington Wolves sign Widnes Vikings hooker|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/45942896|date=22 October 2018}}</ref>
Walker played 23 games in the 2022 Super League season for Warrington which saw the club finish 11th on the table and narrowly avoid relegation. Walker played 28 games for Warrington in the 2023 Super League season as Warrington finished sixth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in the clubs elimination playoff loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/23827325.highs-lows-warrington-wolves-2023-season/|title=Highs and lows from Warrington Wolves' 2023 season|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk|date=3 October 2023 }}</ref>
On 8 June 2024, Walker played in Warrington's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] loss against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Walker played 27 matches for Warrington in the 2024 Super League season as the club reached the semi-final before losing to Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/24654682.warrington-wolves-2024-season-review-february-march/|title=Warrington Wolves 2024 season review, February and March|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref>

==International career==
In July 2018 he was selected in the [[England Knights]] Performance squad.<ref name="Ten new additions in England Knights squad">{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/ten-new-additions-in-england-knights-squad/|title=Ten new additions in England Knights squad|publisher=loverugbyleague|date=24 July 2018|access-date=31 October 2019}}</ref> Later that year he was selected for the England Knights on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Oil Search National Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|access-date=30 October 2019}}</ref>
